headlines: New Zealand police confirm that a bus carrying 27 Chinese tourists crashed in bad weather near Rotorua and left at least five dead and six injured.
A US federal judge rules in favour of 23 citizens who sued after being placed on a terrorism watchlist, saying the list violates their constitutional rights.
2020 Democratic Party presidential debates and forums

events.text: Nicotine marketing
Michigan becomes the first state in the United States to ban the sale of flavored electronic cigarettes, which the state government says are marketed towards children.
New Zealand police confirm that a bus carrying 27 Chinese tourists crashed in bad weather near Rotorua and left at least five dead and six injured.
Hurricane Dorian
Hurricane Dorian increases to a Category Three storm as it approaches The Carolinas in the United States.
At least 20 people are known to have been killed by Hurricane Dorian in the Bahamas.
Hurricane Dorian–Alabama controversy

U.S. President Donald Trump uses an altered map showing the projected forecast of Hurricane Dorian in order to support a disputed claim that Alabama has recently been threatened by the storm. If deliberate, the action is a federal offense.
2019 Samoa assassination plot
After an assassination plot to kill Samoan Prime Minister Tuilaepa A. S. Malielegaoi is foiled, police are working to extradite a Samoan man who lives in Brisbane, Australia.
2019 Hong Kong anti-extradition bill protests
Hong Kong Chief Executive Carrie Lam announces the formal withdrawal of the controversial extradition bill.
Privacy concerns regarding Google
Google agrees to pay a record US$170 million penalty to settle accusations that YouTube broke the law when it knowingly tracked and sold advertisements to children, the Federal Trade Commission says.
A US federal judge rules in favour of 23 citizens who sued after being placed on a terrorism watchlist, saying the list violates their constitutional rights.

The House of Commons of the United Kingdom approves a bill to block a no-deal Brexit next month, by a vote of 327 to 299. The bill instructs Prime Minister Boris Johnson to request another Brexit extension if he cannot secure a deal with the European Union in the coming weeks.
The House of Commons rejects Prime Minister Boris Johnson's motion to hold a general election in October amid continuing political deadlock over Brexit.
Politics of Turkey
Turkish President Recep Tayyip Erdoğan says he cannot accept that his country is not allowed to possess nuclear weapons.
2020 Democratic Party presidential debates and forums
CNN hosts a seven-hour town hall on climate change featuring ten Democratic presidential candidates. An official debate sponsored by the Democratic Party had previously been shot down by the DNC.
